content.problemStatement:
You are given a matrix CCC that has NNN rows and MMM columns where each entry is 111 or 000 or x\texttt{x}x. Here x\texttt{x}x denotes a `don't care` value.

You can perform the following operation on the matrix:


Pick any column jjj such that 1â‰¤jâ‰¤M1 \le j \le M1â‰¤jâ‰¤M.
Assign Ci,jâ†’Ci,jâŠ•1C_{i, j} \to C_{i, j} \oplus 1Ci,jâ€‹â†’Ci,jâ€‹âŠ•1 for all 1â‰¤iâ‰¤N1 \le i \le N1â‰¤iâ‰¤N
You are given that:


0âŠ•1=10 \oplus 1 = 10âŠ•1=1
1âŠ•1=01 \oplus 1 = 01âŠ•1=0
xâŠ•1=x\texttt{x} \oplus 1 = \texttt{x}xâŠ•1=x
where âŠ•\oplusâŠ• denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
You have to perform any number of operations (possibly 000) such that each row in the matrix has **at most** a single 111.

Determine if it's possible to achieve this. Print `YES` if it's possible, else print `NO`.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case has two integers NNN and MMM - the number of rows and number of columns respectively.
The next MMM lines of the test case contain NNN characters each where the jjj-th character in the iii-th line denotes Ci,jC_{i, j}Ci,jâ€‹.


Output Format
Print `YES` if it's possible to perform any number of operations so that each row has at most a single 111, else print `NO`.


You may print each character of `Yes` and `No` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `YES` will be considered identical).
constraints: Sum of Nâ‹…MN \cdot MNâ‹…M over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case-1: We just perform one operation on the first column.
Test case-2: We perform the operations on the columns 1,31, 31,3 and 555.
Test case-3: It can be proven that it is impossible to satisfy the given conditions using any number of operations.
